description_long: This is a seated stone statue depicting a male figure with a traditional nemes headdress, characteristic of ancient Egyptian sculpture. The figure sits cross-legged on a rectangular base, with both hands resting flat on the knees. The facial features are stylized, with almond-shaped eyes, a straight nose, and a faint smile, all rendered in a smooth, idealized manner. The statue's body is robust and simplified, with minimal detailing on the torso and limbs, emphasizing the monumentality of the form. The base of the sculpture appears to have inscriptions or carvings, possibly hieroglyphs, along its front edge. The entire piece is carved from a single block of dark gray stone, giving it a solid and enduring presence.
description: A seated stone statue of a male figure in a nemes headdress, with hands on knees and stylized facial features, carved from dark gray stone. The figure sits on a rectangular base with possible inscriptions.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face, headdress, hands on knees, and inscriptions on the base.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, the drape of the headdress, and the arm's alignment along the body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the headdress, shoulders, and the smooth, unadorned back of the statue.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the face, the headdress, and the arm's position, mirroring the left side.
attribute_subject_matter: seated human figure in ancient Egyptian style
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture is shown isolated on a neutral background.
